#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Nurge installer
# Usage:
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash -s -- --pre
#   curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash -s -- v0.1.0

REPO="usenurge/nurge"
INSTALL_DIR="/usr/local/bin"
BINARY="nurge"

# --- Helpers ---

info()  { printf "\033[1;34m→\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
ok()    { printf "\033[1;32m✓\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
warn()  { printf "\033[1;33m!\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
fail()  { printf "\033[1;31m✗\033[0m %s\n" "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

# --- Detect platform ---

detect_os() {
  case "$(uname -s)" in
    Linux*)  echo "linux" ;;
    Darwin*) echo "darwin" ;;
    *)       fail "Unsupported OS: $(uname -s)" ;;
  esac
}

detect_arch() {
  case "$(uname -m)" in
    x86_64|amd64)  echo "amd64" ;;
    arm64|aarch64) echo "arm64" ;;
    *)             fail "Unsupported architecture: $(uname -m)" ;;
  esac
}

# --- Resolve version ---

resolve_version() {
  local requested="${1:-}"
  local pre="${2:-false}"

  if [ -n "$requested" ] && [ "$requested" != "--pre" ]; then
    echo "$requested"
    return
  fi

  info "Fetching latest release..."
  local releases
  releases=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/${REPO}/releases?per_page=20") || fail "Failed to fetch releases"

  local version=""
  if [ "$pre" = "true" ]; then
    version=$(echo "$releases" | grep -o '"tag_name": *"[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"tag_name": *"//;s/"//')
  else
    version=$(echo "$releases" | grep -o '"tag_name": *"[^"]*"' | grep -v 'alpha\|beta\|rc' | head -1 | sed 's/.*"tag_name": *"//;s/"//')
  fi

  [ -z "$version" ] && fail "No release found"
  echo "$version"
}

# --- Download & install ---

install() {
  local version="$1"
  local os="$2"
  local arch="$3"

  local archive_name="${BINARY}_${os}_${arch}"
  local ext="tar.gz"
  [ "$os" = "windows" ] && ext="zip"

  local url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${version}/${archive_name}.${ext}"

  info "Downloading ${BINARY} ${version} (${os}/${arch})..."
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
  trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

  curl -fsSL "$url" -o "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" || fail "Download failed. Check that ${version} exists at https://github.com/${REPO}/releases"

  info "Extracting..."
  if [ "$ext" = "tar.gz" ]; then
    tar -xzf "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" -C "$tmpdir"
  else
    unzip -q "${tmpdir}/archive.${ext}" -d "$tmpdir"
  fi

  # Find the binary (may be nested in a directory)
  local bin_path
  bin_path=$(find "$tmpdir" -name "$BINARY" -type f | head -1)
  [ -z "$bin_path" ] && fail "Binary not found in archive"

  chmod +x "$bin_path"

  # Install to target directory
  if [ -w "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    mv "$bin_path" "${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
  else
    info "Need sudo to install to ${INSTALL_DIR}"
    sudo mv "$bin_path" "${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
  fi

  ok "Installed ${BINARY} ${version} to ${INSTALL_DIR}/${BINARY}"
}

# --- Main ---

main() {
  local arg="${1:-}"
  local pre=false

  if [ "$arg" = "--pre" ]; then
    pre=true
    arg=""
  fi

  local os arch version
  os=$(detect_os)
  arch=$(detect_arch)
  version=$(resolve_version "$arg" "$pre")

  install "$version" "$os" "$arch"

  # Post-install: next steps
  echo ""
  echo "  Get started:"
  echo "    nurge my-outbound"
  echo "    cd my-outbound"
  echo ""
}

main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO="usenurge/nurge"
BINARY="nurge"
INSTALL_DIR="/usr/local/bin"

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

info() { echo -e "${BOLD}$1${NC}"; }
success() { echo -e "${GREEN}$1${NC}"; }
warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}$1${NC}"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}Error: $1${NC}" >&2; exit 1; }

# Parse arguments
CHANNEL="stable"
VERSION=""
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --pre|--prerelease) CHANNEL="pre" ;;
        v*) VERSION="$arg" ;;
        *) error "Unknown argument: $arg. Usage: install.sh [--pre] [vX.Y.Z]" ;;
    esac
done

# Detect OS
OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
case "$OS" in
    darwin) OS="darwin" ;;
    linux)  OS="linux" ;;
    mingw*|msys*|cygwin*) error "Use PowerShell on Windows: irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/$REPO/main/install.ps1 | iex" ;;
    *) error "Unsupported OS: $OS" ;;
esac

# Detect architecture
ARCH=$(uname -m)
case "$ARCH" in
    x86_64|amd64)  ARCH="amd64" ;;
    arm64|aarch64) ARCH="arm64" ;;
    *) error "Unsupported architecture: $ARCH" ;;
esac

# Get version
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    info "Fetching latest version..."

    if [ "$CHANNEL" = "stable" ]; then
        # Only stable releases (no -alpha, -beta, -rc)
        VERSION=$(curl -sSL "https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases/latest" 2>/dev/null | grep '"tag_name"' | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/' || true)
        if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo ""
            warn "No stable release available yet."
            echo "  To install the latest dev release:"
            echo "  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/$REPO/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --pre"
            echo ""
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        # Latest release including pre-releases
        VERSION=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases" | grep '"tag_name"' | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
        if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
            error "No releases found at https://github.com/$REPO/releases"
        fi
        warn "Installing pre-release: $VERSION"
    fi
fi

# Strip leading 'v' for filename
VERSION_NUM="${VERSION#v}"

FILENAME="nurge_${VERSION_NUM}_${OS}_${ARCH}.tar.gz"
URL="https://github.com/$REPO/releases/download/$VERSION/$FILENAME"

info "Installing nurge $VERSION ($OS/$ARCH)..."

# Download and extract
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT

curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$TMPDIR/$FILENAME" || error "Download failed. Check that $VERSION exists at https://github.com/$REPO/releases"
tar -xzf "$TMPDIR/$FILENAME" -C "$TMPDIR"

# Install
if [ -w "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    mv "$TMPDIR/$BINARY" "$INSTALL_DIR/$BINARY"
else
    info "Need sudo to install to $INSTALL_DIR"
    sudo mv "$TMPDIR/$BINARY" "$INSTALL_DIR/$BINARY"
fi

chmod +x "$INSTALL_DIR/$BINARY"

# Verify
if command -v nurge &>/dev/null; then
    success "nurge installed successfully!"
    echo ""
    info "Get started:"
    echo "  nurge init"
else
    error "Installation completed but 'nurge' not found in PATH. Add $INSTALL_DIR to your PATH."
fi

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO="usenurge/nurge"
BINARY="nurge"
INSTALL_DIR="/usr/local/bin"

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

info() { echo -e "${BOLD}$1${NC}"; }
success() { echo -e "${GREEN}$1${NC}"; }
warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}$1${NC}"; }
error() { echo -e "${RED}Error: $1${NC}" >&2; exit 1; }

# Parse arguments
CHANNEL="stable"
VERSION=""
for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --pre|--prerelease) CHANNEL="pre" ;;
        v*) VERSION="$arg" ;;
        *) error "Unknown argument: $arg. Usage: install.sh [--pre] [vX.Y.Z]" ;;
    esac
done

# Detect OS
OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
case "$OS" in
    darwin) OS="darwin" ;;
    linux)  OS="linux" ;;
    mingw*|msys*|cygwin*) error "Use PowerShell on Windows: irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/$REPO/main/install.ps1 | iex" ;;
    *) error "Unsupported OS: $OS" ;;
esac

# Detect architecture
ARCH=$(uname -m)
case "$ARCH" in
    x86_64|amd64)  ARCH="amd64" ;;
    arm64|aarch64) ARCH="arm64" ;;
    *) error "Unsupported architecture: $ARCH" ;;
esac

# Get version
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    info "Fetching latest version..."

    if [ "$CHANNEL" = "stable" ]; then
        # Only stable releases (no -alpha, -beta, -rc)
        VERSION=$(curl -sSL "https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases/latest" 2>/dev/null | grep '"tag_name"' | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/' || true)
        if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo ""
            warn "No stable release available yet."
            echo "  To install the latest dev release:"
            echo "  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/$REPO/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --pre"
            echo ""
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        # Latest release including pre-releases
        VERSION=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases" | grep '"tag_name"' | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
        if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
            error "No releases found at https://github.com/$REPO/releases"
        fi
        warn "Installing pre-release: $VERSION"
    fi
fi

# Strip leading 'v' for filename
VERSION_NUM="${VERSION#v}"

FILENAME="nurge_${VERSION_NUM}_${OS}_${ARCH}.tar.gz"
URL="https://github.com/$REPO/releases/download/$VERSION/$FILENAME"

info "Installing nurge $VERSION ($OS/$ARCH)..."

# Download and extract
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT

curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$TMPDIR/$FILENAME" || error "Download failed. Check that $VERSION exists at https://github.com/$REPO/releases"
tar -xzf "$TMPDIR/$FILENAME" -C "$TMPDIR"

# Install
if [ -w "$INSTALL_DIR" ]; then
    mv "$TMPDIR/$BINARY" "$INSTALL_DIR/$BINARY"
else
    info "Need sudo to install to $INSTALL_DIR"
    sudo mv "$TMPDIR/$BINARY" "$INSTALL_DIR/$BINARY"
fi

chmod +x "$INSTALL_DIR/$BINARY"

# Verify
if command -v nurge &>/dev/null; then
    success "nurge installed successfully!"
    echo ""
    info "Get started:"
    echo "  nurge init"
else
    error "Installation completed but 'nurge' not found in PATH. Add $INSTALL_DIR to your PATH."
fi

## Key Design Decisions

- **CLI-as-API:** Skills never touch the database directly. All data flows through `nurge` subcommands with `--format json`. This keeps the Go binary as the single source of truth.
- **No HTTP server:** Everything is local CLI. No daemon, no ports, no API server.
- **Idempotent init:** Every step in `nurge` checks if already done before executing. Safe to re-run.
- **Old wizard is superseded:** The charmbracelet/huh-based setup wizard is replaced by the two-phase approach. Don't extend the old wizard.
- **Learn from every session:** Users are GTM people, not developers. They won't commit or version anything. Nurge captures conversation learnings and skill versions in the DB automatically. Skills get smarter over time without user effort.
- **DB owns versioning, not git:** Skill content lives on disk as SKILL.md (for Claude Code), but the DB tracks all versions, rollback history, and what data each version was generated from. Users never interact with git for this.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What is Nurge

Nurge is an outbound sales tool that learns from your sent email history. It runs as a Go CLI binary (`nurge`) that handles deterministic operations (sync, store, serve) while delegating intelligence (classify, converse, generate) to Claude via Claude Code skills.

**Core principle:** Go binary = dumb (sync, store, serve). Claude = smart (classify, converse, generate).

## Installation

Users install via `curl` from the Cloudflare Worker at `install.nurge.sh`:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash
```

The worker (`workers/install/`) proxies the install script from `usenurge/nurge` (public repo). After install, users run:

```
nurge my-outbound
cd my-outbound
```

`nurge <directory>` creates the folder and initializes everything inside it. Running `nurge` with no argument initializes in the current directory (must be empty). Refuses to init in non-empty directories unless `--force` is passed. `nurge init` still works as a hidden alias.

## Architecture: Two-Phase Setup

### Phase A: `nurge` (Go binary, deterministic)
Everything that doesn't need AI. One command, ~5 seconds, zero interaction:
1. Create `.nurge/` directory and config files
2. Create SQLite database
3. Update `.gitignore`
4. Install Claude Code hooks
5. Install setup skill to `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/`
6. Git init + initial commit
7. Print "run /nurge:setup" or launch Claude

**Idempotent** — re-running skips completed steps.

**Smart next-step detection:**
- Inside Claude Code? → "Run /nurge:setup"
- Claude not installed? → "Install Claude Code first"
- Terminal with Claude available? → "Open Claude Code and run /nurge:setup"

### Phase B: `/nurge:setup` (Claude Code skill, conversational)
Everything that benefits from AI reasoning. Interactive conversation, ~5 minutes:
1. Connect Gmail → Claude runs `nurge connect gmail`, guides through OAuth
2. Connect CRM (optional) → asks if user has HubSpot, connects if yes
3. Privacy consent → "Send email subjects to Claude? Continue?"
4. Sync emails → `nurge sync` (all sent, last 90 days)
5. Classify → Claude reads emails in batches, classifies, asks user about uncertain ones (~95% accuracy)
6. Product discovery → "What's your website?" → WebFetch → confirms
7. ICP conversation → Claude infers ICP from email patterns, confirms
8. Analyze patterns → `nurge context generate` → presents insights
9. DNS/health check → `dig TXT` + `nurge emails stats`
10. Generate 17 skills → Claude writes project-local skills from data
11. Summary + insights → "Try: write a cold email to the CTO at DataStack"

## Conversation Learning System

Nurge gets smarter with every Claude Code session. Users are GTM employees and founders — they don't use git, so Nurge must capture and version everything internally.

### How it works
1. **Capture** — after each Claude Code session, export the conversation into the DB (tone feedback, rejected drafts, ICP refinements, preferences)
2. **Learn** — distill sessions into persistent learnings that skills reference
3. **Improve** — skills get better with every conversation, not just every email sync

### What gets captured
- Tone preferences ("too salesy", "more casual", "shorter")
- Rejected and accepted drafts
- ICP refinements ("we don't sell to startups under 50 people")
- Product positioning tweaks ("don't mention pricing in cold emails")
- Competitor intel mentioned in conversation
- Any explicit user corrections

### Skill versioning
Skills are stored as SKILL.md files on disk (for Claude Code to read), but **full versions are tracked in the DB**:
- Content — the actual SKILL.md text
- Version number — auto-incrementing per skill
- Generated from — email count, classification state, learnings applied
- Timestamp
- Active flag — which version is currently on disk

This lets Nurge roll back if a regeneration makes things worse, show what changed between versions, and know when skills are stale.

### CLI commands
```
nurge learn save --stdin               # Skill self-reports session learnings
nurge learn list --format json         # List all learnings
nurge skills list --format json        # List skills with version info
nurge skills history <name>            # Version history for a skill
nurge skills rollback <name> <version> # Restore a previous skill version
```

## Skill ↔ CLI Interface

All skill-to-binary communication is via CLI. No HTTP API, no direct DB access:

```
nurge account                          # Show auth status
nurge account login                    # Force re-auth via browser
nurge account logout                   # Revoke token + delete local auth
nurge emails list --format json        # Skill reads data
nurge emails classify --stdin          # Skill writes data
nurge config show --format json        # Skill reads config
nurge config set icp.titles "CTO,VP Eng"  # Skill writes config
nurge context generate --format json   # Skill gets insights
nurge send                             # Skill sends email
nurge log --class outbound             # Classification filters
nurge emails stats                     # Email statistics
nurge emails check                     # Email health check
nurge learn save --stdin               # Save session learnings
nurge learn list --format json         # List learnings
nurge skills list --format json        # List skills with versions
nurge skills history <name>            # Skill version history
nurge skills rollback <name> <version> # Restore previous version
nurge connectors                       # List connected services
nurge connectors available             # List all known connectors
nurge connectors info <id> --format json  # Show connector details
nurge connectors validate <file>       # Validate a connector YAML
nurge connectors add --file <path>     # Add a custom connector YAML
nurge connect <id>                     # Connect a service
nurge disconnect <id>                  # Disconnect a service
```

## The 17 Generated Skills

All written to `.claude/skills/nurge-*/SKILL.md` (project-local, not global). See `docs/skills.md` for full documentation.

| Category  | Skills |
|-----------|--------|
| Context   | `nurge-product`, `nurge-icp`, `nurge-personas` |
| Research  | `nurge-research`, `nurge-qualify` |
| Workflow  | `nurge-pipeline` (orchestrates everything) |
| Action    | `nurge-outbound`, `nurge-sequence`, `nurge-followup`, `nurge-replies`, `nurge-book` |
| Prospects | `nurge-prospects` (list building, segmentation — the CRM for fresh users) |
| Health    | `nurge-hygiene`, `nurge-sending-health` (includes warmup management) |
| Measure   | `nurge-analytics`, `nurge-review` (periodic coaching & KPI review) |

## Tech Stack

### Go Binary
- **CLI framework:** [cobra](https://github.com/spf13/cobra) with [viper](https://github.com/spf13/viper) for config
- **Database:** SQLite via [mattn/go-sqlite3](https://github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3) (requires CGO_ENABLED=1 and gcc)
  - Use WAL journal mode and `_busy_timeout` for concurrent access
  - DSN format: `file:.nurge/nurge.db?_journal_mode=WAL&_busy_timeout=5000&_fk=true`
- **Connectors** (YAML-defined, pluggable):
  - Definitions in `internal/connector/definitions/*.yaml` (bundled) and `.nurge/connectors/definitions/*.yaml` (project-local)
  - Auth methods: OAuth2, API key, basic auth, webhook — all handled by a generic engine
  - Three tiers: Tier 1 (email, Go sync), Tier 2 (CRM, Claude-native), Tier 3 (enrichment, Claude-native)
  - Adding a connector = dropping a YAML file, no Go code needed
  - All tokens stored in `.nurge/connectors/<id>/token.json`
- **Terminal UI (old wizard, superseded):** [charmbracelet/huh](https://github.com/charmbracelet/huh) — was used for the old setup wizard TUI forms. Keep huh only if needed for non-init interactive prompts.

### Claude Code Integration
- **Skills:** SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `version`)
  - Global skills: `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/SKILL.md`
  - Project skills: `.claude/skills/nurge-*/SKILL.md`
- **Hooks:** Configured in `.claude/settings.json` under event keys (`PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `Stop`, `SessionStart`)
  - Hook types: `command` (runs shell command) or `prompt` (injects prompt)
  - Matcher patterns filter which tools trigger the hook
- **Slash commands:** Markdown files with optional YAML frontmatter (`description`, `argument-hint`, `allowed-tools`)
  - Support `$ARGUMENTS`, `$1`/`$2` positional args, `@path/to/file` references, `` !`command` `` bash execution

## Build & Run

```bash
# Build (dev — quick, no version stamp)
go build -o nurge .

# Build (versioned — for local testing, always use this when installing)
COMMIT=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD) && \
DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S) && \
go build -ldflags "-X main.version=dev-$(date +%Y%m%d)-${COMMIT} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}" -o nurge .

# Install to PATH (after versioned build)
cp ./nurge /usr/local/bin/nurge

# Verify installed version
nurge version
# → nurge dev-20260323-c4e6c6d (commit: c4e6c6d, built: 2026-03-23T14:18:11)

# Run
./nurge my-outbound
./nurge sync
./nurge emails list --format json

# Test
go test ./...
go test ./internal/gmail/ -run TestSync -v    # single test

# Lint
golangci-lint run
```

## Project Layout

```
cmd/              # Cobra command definitions (root.go, init.go, sync.go, connect.go, emails.go, config.go, account.go, etc.)
internal/         # Private packages
  auth/           # Account auth — EnsureAuth, Login, Logout, Convex HTTP client, token file (~/.nurge/auth.json)
  browser/        # Shared cross-platform browser.Open(url)
  callbackserver/ # Shared local HTTP callback server for OAuth flows
  connector/      # Connector interface, registry, shared OAuth flow, per-connector implementations
  db/             # SQLite schema, migrations, queries
  classifier/     # Email classification (outbound, internal, personal, etc.)
  config/         # Config read/write (.nurge/config.json)
  hooks/          # Claude Code hook installer
  skills/         # Skill template generator
  learning/       # Session learning capture, distillation, and retrieval
.nurge/           # Project data directory (gitignored)
  nurge.db        # SQLite database
  config.json     # Project configuration
  connectors/     # Per-connector auth state (gmail/, hubspot/)
```

## Key Design Decisions

- **CLI-as-API:** Skills never touch the database directly. All data flows through `nurge` subcommands with `--format json`. This keeps the Go binary as the single source of truth.
- **No HTTP server:** Everything is local CLI. No daemon, no ports, no API server.
- **Idempotent init:** Every step in `nurge` checks if already done before executing. Safe to re-run.
- **Old wizard is superseded:** The charmbracelet/huh-based setup wizard is replaced by the two-phase approach. Don't extend the old wizard.
- **Learn from every session:** Users are GTM people, not developers. They won't commit or version anything. Nurge captures conversation learnings and skill versions in the DB automatically. Skills get smarter over time without user effort.
- **DB owns versioning, not git:** Skill content lives on disk as SKILL.md (for Claude Code), but the DB tracks all versions, rollback history, and what data each version was generated from. Users never interact with git for this.
